chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P.No.S-1403 of 2023 Date Order with signature of the Judge 1. For order on office objections 2. For hearing of CMA 10324/2023. 3. For hearing of main case 12.08.2024. Mr. Shujaat Abbas, Advocate for petitioner Mr. S.M. Jehangir a/w Muhammad Aslam, Advocate for respondent No.1. = This petition has challenged an order dated 20.12.2023 whereby learned Family Judge, while deciding an application u/s 12 of Guardian and Wards Act has allowed the petitioner to see minors on every second and fourth Saturday of each month besides allowing him to see her children for three days in winter vacation subject to consent of the minors. The case of petitioner is that this condition is not in accordance with law. Learned counsel for respondent is present alongwith respondent/father. They both submit that this condition has been enforced because both the minors do not want to go to the house of their mother. The daughter is of 14 years and son is of 10 years. They both cannot be compelled to see their mother against their wish. However, they both have agreed that if the petitioner proposes some neutral venue like McDonald Restaurant situated at Malir Cantt. Road, the meeting can be arranged, and minors can be taken there for meeting purpose. This proposal has been agreed by learned counsel for petitioner. Accordingly, this petition is disposed of with consent in the terms whereby on every Sunday, respondent/father would bring the minors at McDonald Restaurant, Malir Cantt. Road to meet with the petitioner from 12.00 pm to 2.00 p.m instead in the court premises as ordered by the Family Court.

Accordingly, this petition is disposed of with consent in the terms whereby on every Sunday, respondent/father would bring the minors at McDonald Restaurant, Malir Cantt. Road to meet with the petitioner from 12.00 pm to 2.00 p.m instead in the court premises as ordered by the Family Court. This arrangement shall continue till final decision on application u/s 25 of Guardian & Wards Act which learned Family court is directed to conclude and decide within a period of two months without fail. The petition stands disposed of alongwith listed application. JUDGE A.K
